coreQuestion: What really happens between typing a URL and seeing a web page?
coreTension: Speed vs. security — every layer of the web stack trades latency for reliability, and understanding that tension unlocks real optimization.

sections.title: The Web Request Lifecycle
sections.subtitle: From URL to pixels: the full journey of a web request
sections.bridgeTo: Before a browser can talk to a server, it needs to find the server's address. That's where DNS comes in.

sections.title: DNS: The Internet's Phone Book
sections.subtitle: How domain names become IP addresses
sections.bridgeTo: Now that the browser knows the server's IP address, it needs a common language to request resources. Enter HTTP.

sections.title: HTTP: The Language of the Web
sections.subtitle: The protocol that powers every web interaction
sections.bridgeTo: HTTP defines what to say, but how do those messages actually travel across the network? TCP/IP handles the delivery.

sections.title: TCP/IP: Reliable Delivery
sections.subtitle: Breaking data into packets and reassembling them perfectly
sections.bridgeTo: Reliable delivery is essential, but so is privacy. TLS wraps TCP connections in encryption to keep data safe.

sections.title: TLS: Securing the Connection
sections.subtitle: Encryption, certificates, and trust on the web
sections.bridgeTo: With a secure channel established, the request finally reaches the server. What happens when it arrives?

sections.title: Server-Side Processing
sections.subtitle: From incoming request to outgoing response
sections.bridgeTo: The server has crafted a response full of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Now the browser must turn those bytes into a visual page.

sections.title: The Browser Rendering Engine
sections.subtitle: Parsing, layout, and painting pixels to the screen
sections.bridgeTo: Rendering works great for one user, but what about millions? CDNs solve the scale problem.

sections.title: CDNs & Edge Computing
sections.subtitle: Bringing content closer to users worldwide
sections.bridgeTo: CDNs move data closer, but caching is what makes repeated access nearly instantaneous.

sections.title: Caching: Speed Through Memory
sections.subtitle: Storing responses to avoid redundant work
sections.bridgeTo: Caching is one optimization strategy, but there is a whole toolkit of techniques to make the web feel instant.

sections.title: Web Performance Optimization
sections.subtitle: Measuring and improving what users actually experience
sections.bridgeTo: Performance tooling lives inside browsers. Understanding browser engines reveals why different browsers behave differently.

sections.title: Browser Engines & Standards
sections.subtitle: The software that interprets the web
sections.bridgeTo: With every layer explored, we can now see the full picture of how the web works as an integrated system.

sections.title: The Complete Picture
sections.subtitle: Integrating every layer into a unified mental model
